memoryArtifacts.description:
ALEXANDER WHITE was born about 1550 of Sturton le Steeple, Nottinghamshire, England, to Thomas White (1524-1579) and Mary MacLean (1518-.)  He married Eleanor Smith.

Alexander White died 6 May 1596, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Smith於1492年出生在英國賀福爾時，他的父親Thomas de Eley Smythe32歲，母親Lady Anne le Strange30歲。他和Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske至少有1個兒子。他去世於1556年6月5日在英國林可爾史爾維爾布享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾霍爾亞其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Smith于1492年出生在英国賀福爾时，他的父亲Thomas de Eley Smythe32岁，母亲Lady Anne le Strange30岁。他和Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske至少有1个儿子。他去世于1556年6月5日在英国林可爾史爾維爾布享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾霍爾亞其。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Smith was born in 1492, in Herefordshire, England, his father, Thomas de Eley Smythe, was 32 and his mother, Lady Anne le Strange, was 30. He had at least 1 son with Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske. He died on 5 June 1556, in Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Holbeach, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Smith est né en 1492, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas d’Eley Smythe, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne le Strange, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske. Il est décédé le 5 juin 1556, à Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Holbeach, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Smith 1492 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Eley Smythe 32 und seine Mutter Lady Anne le Strange 30. Er hatte mit Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 5. Juni 1556 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Holbeach, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Smith è nato il nel 1492 nel Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Eley Smythe, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Lady Anne le Strange, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske. Morì il 5 giugno 1556, nel Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Holbeach, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Smithが1492年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Thomas de Eley Smytheは32歳，母Lady Anne le Strangeは30歳であった。彼はAgnes Smythe Hulcock Wraskeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1556年6月5日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウエルボウルンで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアホールベアチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Smith(이)가 1492년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Eley Smythe(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Anne le Strange(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1556년 6월 5일 영국 링코실르 웰보우른에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 홀브이치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Smith nasceu em 1492, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Eley Smythe, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lady Anne le Strange, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske. Ele faleceu em 5 de junho de 1556, em Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Holbeach, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Smith родился в 1492 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Eley Smythe было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne le Strange, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske. Он умер 5 июня 1556 г. в местности велбурн, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности холбич, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Smith nació en 1492, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Eley Smythe, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lady Anne le Strange, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Agnes Smythe Hulcock Wraske. Murió el 5 de junio de 1556, en Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Holbeach, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
